title: Aloo Gobi

seo.title: How to Make Aloo Gobi — Easy Pakistani Recipe
seo.metaDescription: Learn how to make authentic Aloo Gobi. This easy Pakistani recipe includes step-by-step instructions, tips, and serving ideas.
h2Headings: Pro Tips for the Best Results
seo.imageCaption: Delicious homemade Aloo Gobi — a beloved Pakistani classic

description: Aloo Gobi is a classic Pakistani vegetarian dish featuring tender potatoes and cauliflower florets cooked in a fragrant blend of spices. This comforting and wholesome curry is a staple in many Pakistani homes, perfect for a hearty meal. Enjoy its rich flavors and simple preparation.

instructions.title: Prepare Vegetables
instructions.text: Wash and chop the potatoes into 1-inch cubes. Cut the cauliflower into medium-sized florets. Keep them separate as they cook at different rates. Chop the onion and tomatoes finely, and prepare ginger and garlic pastes.

instructions.title: Saute Aromatics
instructions.text: Heat oil in a large pot or karahi over medium heat. Add the chopped onion and saute until it turns light golden brown, about 5-7 minutes. Add ginger paste and garlic paste, and cook for another minute until fragrant.

instructions.title: Add Spices and Tomatoes
instructions.text: Stir in the chopped tomatoes and green chilies. Cook until the tomatoes soften and the oil starts to separate from the masala, about 5 minutes. Now add turmeric powder, red chili powder, coriander powder, cumin powder, and salt. Mix well and cook for 2-3 minutes, adding a splash of water if the masala sticks.

instructions.title: Cook Potatoes
instructions.text: Add the cubed potatoes to the masala. Mix thoroughly to coat the potatoes with the spices. Add about 1/2 cup of water, cover the pot, and let the potatoes cook on low heat for 10 minutes, or until they are halfway tender.

instructions.title: Incorporate Cauliflower
instructions.text: Now add the cauliflower florets to the pot. Gently mix them with the potatoes and masala. Cover the pot again and continue to cook on low heat for another 10-15 minutes, or until both the potatoes and cauliflower are fully tender. Stir occasionally to prevent sticking.

instructions.title: Finish and Garnish
instructions.text: Once the vegetables are tender, uncover the pot. Increase heat slightly if there is excess water to dry it out. Stir in the garam masala. Garnish with fresh chopped coriander. Serve hot with roti, naan, or rice.

tips: To ensure even cooking, try to cut your potatoes and cauliflower florets into similar sizes. You can par-boil the potatoes slightly before adding them to speed up cooking and ensure they dont break apart.
For a richer flavor, toast the whole cumin seeds in a dry pan before grinding them into powder. This technique enhances the aroma significantly.
Aloo Gobi tastes even better the next day as the flavors meld together. Store leftovers in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 3 days.
article: Aloo Gobi is a beloved Pakistani Vegetarian dish enjoyed across Pakistan. This authentic recipe brings traditional flavors to your kitchen.
